title: ochrosperma citriodorum
intro: Ochrosperma citriodorum, also known as Lemon-scented Myrtle, is a shrub native to Australia that belongs to the family Myrtaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Aboriginal medicine, where it is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ochrosperma citriodorum include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve respiratory issues, and exhibit antimicrobial activity against various pathogens. Ochrosperma citriodorum is often prepared as a tea, infused oil, or dried leaves for use in herbal remedies, and its essential oil can be used in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and well-being.
